plan_metrics.plan_content:
## Plan Summary

**Root cause:** Classic off-by-one error in `src/utils.ts`, line 7.

### The bug
```typescript
// loops while i < items.length - 1 → skips the last element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

### The fix — one character change
```typescript
// loops while i < items.length → processes every element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

### Why it breaks
For an array like `["hello", "world", "foo"]` (length = 3), the condition `i < 3 - 1` evaluates to `i < 2`, so the loop only visits indices 0 and 1 — `"foo"` is never processed.

### Tests
The existing Vitest suite in `tests/utils.test.ts` already has tests covering this (e.g., `"should process all items in the array"`, `"should handle a single item"`, `"should handle two items"`). All of those are currently failing and will pass after the fix. Verified by running `npm test`.
